summary: Kenneth Suna discusses why 3x leveraged ETFs like UPRO appear attractive (30% annual returns vs 10% for VOO) but are fundamentally unsuitable for long-term investing due to compounding losses, high fees (1%+), and the mathematical impossibility of recovery after significant drawdowns. The video is educational risk-management content, not an options strategy.
takeaways: 3x leveraged ETFs charge fees over 1% vs near-zero for index funds like VOO
A 20% market drop causes 60% loss in 3x leveraged funds; recovery needs 150% gain vs 25% for unleveraged
After a 60% loss, even a 30% market recovery only brings position to 60% of original value
A 30% market crash in leveraged funds equals 90% loss, compounding previous losses irreversibly
Mathematical recovery requirement: 1,462% gain needed after COVID-style 30% crash following prior losses, vs 37% for VOO
title: Make 30% a year???  LEVERAGED ETFs
